The Fault in Our Stars (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $124,872,350Details
International Box Office $182,294,484
Worldwide Box Office $307,166,834
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $27,040,320
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $17,980,614
Total $45,020,934
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Hazel and Gus are two teenagers who share an acerbic wit, a disdain for the conventional, and a love that sweeps them on a journey. Their relationship is all the more miraculous given that Hazel’s other constant companion is an oxygen tank, Gus jokes about his prosthetic leg, and they met and fell in love at a cancer support group.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$48,002,523 (38.4% of total gross)
Legs:2.60 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:40.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$12,000,000 (worldwide box office is 25.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,173 opening theaters/3,340 max. theaters, 5.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $182,799,666

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: June 6th, 2014 (Wide) by 20th Century Fox
International Releases: June 4th, 2014 (Wide) (Iceland)
June 5th, 2014 (Wide) (Philippines)
June 5th, 2014 (Wide) (Hungary)
June 5th, 2014 (Wide) (Mexico)
June 5th, 2014 (Wide) (New Zealand)
Video Release: September 16th, 2014 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for thematic elements, some sexuality and brief strong language.
(Rating bulletin 2310, 2/19/2014)
Running Time: 125 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Voiceover/Narration
Plot point: Cancer, Coming of Age, Death of a Son or Daughter, Recluse, Romance, Terminal Illness, Writing and Writers
Subgenre: Romantic Drama
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: 20th Century Fox
Production Countries: United States, Canada, United Kingdom
Languages: English

Synopsis

Hazel and Gus are two teenagers who share an acerbic wit, a disdain for the conventional, and a love that sweeps them on a journey. Their relationship is all the more miraculous given that Hazel’s other constant companion is an oxygen tank, Gus jokes about his prosthetic leg, and they met and fell in love at a cancer support group.
